spec_match.rationale: Successfully demonstrates the letter-spacing intervention operating on a per-run basis within a single paragraph element containing Latin, Arabic, Devanagari, and CJK runs.

interactive.rationale: Provides a letter-spacing range slider mapped dynamically in 'em' units across all sample paragraphs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Directly clarifies the per-run vs per-block nature of the intervention in its descriptive text.

edge_cases.rationale: Presents multiple script blocks side-by-side (Latin-only, Arabic-only, Devanagari-only, mixed) with built-in feature detection log outputs.

references.rationale: Appropriately links to ChromeStatus feature 4885407109742592 in its see also section.

design_system.rationale: Well-designed stage containing nicely colored badges representing different script groups, maintaining consistent and clean brand styling.

openQuestions.title: Explicitly test bidirectionality (bidi) layout interactions
openQuestions.detail: Since mixed paragraphs contain both LTR (Latin/CJK) and RTL (Arabic) text, the interaction of letter-spacing with RTL spacing directions is a complex edge case. Adding a toggle to reverse paragraph direction (dir='rtl' vs dir='ltr') could further demonstrate spec compliance.

summary: A wonderful concept page demonstrating precise per-run application of the letter-spacing rule in mixed script texts, backed by solid fallback probes and visual color codes.
